invalid_events.title: Mt. Gox acquired Bitomat
invalid_events.description: After Bitomat lost wallet data and customer balances, Mt. Gox acquired the service and migrated users in August 2011.
invalid_events.notes: Successor is Mt. Gox.

invalid_events.title: Bter users migrated to Gate.io
invalid_events.description: Gate.io announced support for BTER users to move funds to gate.io, reflecting the completed brand transition.
invalid_events.notes: HEI uses 2017-12-31 as the conservative rebrand marker.

invalid_events.title: Coinbase announced the transition away from Coinbase Pro
invalid_events.description: Coinbase announced it would replace Coinbase Pro with Coinbase Advanced on Coinbase.
invalid_events.notes: Announcement month used conservatively from official help text.

invalid_events.title: Coinbase Pro discontinued
invalid_events.description: Coinbase documentation states Coinbase Pro was discontinued in December 2023 and replaced by Coinbase Advanced.
invalid_events.notes: HEI uses 2023-12-31 as the conservative terminal marker.

invalid_events.title: Bleutrade announced move to Portugal
invalid_events.description: Bleutrade announced a move to Portugal and closure of its Malta office.
invalid_events.notes: Useful lineage context, not the terminal event.

invalid_events.title: Bololex Exchange website became inaccessible
invalid_events.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that the Bololex Exchange website could not be accessed on 2024-09-18 and that there had been no preceding notice of maintenance, migration, or recovery plans.
invalid_events.notes: The inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

invalid_events.title: Bitzlato was required to shut down after founder arrest
invalid_events.description: Public reporting stated that the arrest of Bitzlato's founder and concurrent enforcement action resulted in the platform being required to shut down.
invalid_events.notes: The same-day enforcement action is the immediate operational stop marker.

invalid_events.title: BQT platform showed no sign of active operations
invalid_events.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that the BQT website was up but the exchange could not be accessed and the firm's last visible social activity had been earlier in 2022.
invalid_events.notes: This inaccessible-but-still-online state is the immediate trigger for the later dead-side handling.

invalid_events.title: CoinBene website became inaccessible
invalid_events.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that CoinBene's website could not be reached on 2022-02-01 and that there had been no preceding messages about maintenance, migration, or any replacement site.
invalid_events.notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

invalid_events.title: CoinEgg website became inaccessible
invalid_events.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that CoinEgg's website could not be reached on 2021-07-15 and that there had been no preceding messages on system maintenance, replacement sites, or similar continuity plans.
invalid_events.notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

invalid_events.title: UniDAX website became inaccessible
invalid_events.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that UniDAX could not be accessed on 2021-03-11 and that there was no accompanying information on system maintenance or similar continuity messaging.
invalid_events.notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

invalid_events.title: Rfinex website became inaccessible
invalid_events.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that Rfinex could not be accessed on 2021-04-10 and that there was no information on system maintenance or anything similar.
invalid_events.notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

invalid_events.title: McxNOW suspended trading after support overload
invalid_events.description: McxNOW announced that it would suspend trading for several months because user growth and support requirements had overwhelmed the service, with users told to withdraw funds before the temporary closure.
invalid_events.notes: This is an earlier suspension event, not the terminal marker.

invalid_events.title: CoinPulse suspended trading and deposits
invalid_events.description: CoinPulse's official notice set 2019-02-01 as the date when all trading and deposits would be suspended while withdrawals remained open.
invalid_events.notes: This is modeled separately from the final maintenance / withdrawal-deadline marker.

invalid_events.title: BitMarket.eu disclosed major customer-fund loss
invalid_events.description: The operator disclosed that customer funds had been used for speculation and that nearly 20,000 BTC of customer funds had been lost.
invalid_events.notes: Recorded as the insolvency / balance-freeze marker before final closure.

invalid_events.title: AEX closed withdrawal function amid liquidity pressure
invalid_events.description: AEX reportedly closed the withdrawal function because short-term liquidity problems had not been resolved and the platform could not meet withdrawal demand in the short term.
invalid_events.notes: Recorded as the liquidity / withdrawal freeze marker before the later full service suspension.

invalid_events.title: BitForex halted withdrawals and became inaccessible
invalid_events.description: BitForex users were unable to withdraw or access the platform after large hot-wallet outflows and a period of no communication from the exchange.
invalid_events.notes: Used as the operational failure marker, not as a final death marker.

invalid_events.title: BKEX suspended withdrawals amid police investigation
invalid_events.description: BKEX suspended withdrawals after stating that some platform user funds were involved in money laundering and that it was cooperating with police to collect evidence.
invalid_events.notes: Used as the operational restriction marker. This is not classified as a final death event because no primary formal closure or liquidation notice is included.

invalid_events.title: ZB.com suspended deposits and withdrawals
invalid_events.description: ZB.com announced that deposit and withdrawal services were suspended, citing temporary maintenance / core-application failure; market trackers continued to display this suspension notice.
invalid_events.notes: Used as the operational restriction marker. Not classified as a final death event.

invalid_events.title: CoinFLEX halted withdrawals
invalid_events.description: CoinFLEX suspended customer withdrawals during a liquidity dispute involving a large counterparty later identified by CoinFLEX leadership as Roger Ver.
invalid_events.notes: Used as the critical operating failure marker.

invalid_events.title: QuadrigaCX received creditor protection after founder death and missing-funds crisis
invalid_events.description: QuadrigaCX sought and received creditor protection in Nova Scotia after founder Gerald Cotten's death and the reported inability to access large amounts of customer cryptocurrency.
invalid_events.notes: This marks the practical end of the original exchange service timeline.

invalid_events.title: QuadrigaCX moved into bankruptcy proceedings
invalid_events.description: QuadrigaCX moved from creditor-protection proceedings into bankruptcy proceedings in April 2019 after recovery and restructuring prospects became remote.
invalid_events.notes: Bankruptcy event is recorded separately from the February 2019 creditor-protection event.

invalid_events.title: OSC staff review concluded Quadriga operated as a fraud
invalid_events.description: The Ontario Securities Commission staff review concluded that Quadriga's collapse resulted from fraud by Gerald Cotten and described the case as an old-fashioned fraud wrapped in modern technology.
invalid_events.notes: Fraud characterization is attributed to the OSC staff review and reporting sources.

invalid_events.title: BTC-e service went offline during law-enforcement action
invalid_events.description: BTC-e went offline around the July 2017 law-enforcement action. Public reference material and reporting describe the 2017 outage/seizure period as the end of the original BTC-e service.
invalid_events.notes: The original BTC-e service is treated as dead. WEX-related successor activity is not merged into the canonical BTC-e entity.

invalid_events.title: Alexander Vinnik pleaded guilty to money-laundering conspiracy
invalid_events.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced in May 2024 that Alexander Vinnik pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit money laundering in connection with BTC-e's operation.
invalid_events.notes: Resolution event for Vinnik. This does not imply the exchange itself resumed or that all earlier allegations were adjudicated.

invalid_events.title: SEC filed to dismiss civil action against Coinbase
invalid_events.description: The SEC filed to dismiss its civil enforcement action against Coinbase in February 2025, ending the lawsuit that began in 2023.
invalid_events.notes: Resolution event recorded separately from the 2023 filing because it materially changed Coinbase's regulatory-history timeline.

invalid_events.title: Bitfinex suffered major bitcoin theft
invalid_events.description: Bitfinex suffered a major security breach in August 2016. Public reporting and DOJ materials describe roughly 119,754 bitcoin stolen from the exchange.
invalid_events.notes: The exchange later continued operating; this event records the breach, not a shutdown.

invalid_events.title: U.S. authorities seized bitcoin connected to the 2016 Bitfinex hack
invalid_events.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced arrests and the seizure of more than $3.6 billion in bitcoin connected to the 2016 Bitfinex hack, calling it the department's largest financial seizure at the time.
invalid_events.notes: This is a recovery/law-enforcement event related to the stolen funds. It does not imply Bitfinex was the accused party in the laundering case.

invalid_events.title: BitMEX founders pleaded guilty to Bank Secrecy Act violations
invalid_events.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York announced that Arthur Hayes and Benjamin Delo pleaded guilty to violating the Bank Secrecy Act by willfully failing to establish, implement, and maintain an AML program at BitMEX.
invalid_events.notes: Founder-level criminal resolution. It is recorded separately from company-level events.

invalid_events.title: BitMEX fined after company guilty plea
invalid_events.description: Reuters and WSJ reported that BitMEX was fined $100 million by a U.S. judge after the company pleaded guilty to a Bank Secrecy Act-related charge over anti-money-laundering failures.
invalid_events.notes: Company-level resolution event. It follows earlier civil settlement and founder-level pleas.

invalid_events.title: Nobitex suffered major security breach
invalid_events.description: Nobitex suffered a major security incident in June 2025. Public reporting and blockchain analysis described more than $90 million in crypto assets being transferred to inaccessible addresses in an apparently politically motivated attack.
invalid_events.notes: The attack was claimed by Predatory Sparrow. Attribution beyond the group's own claim is recorded cautiously.

invalid_events.title: Altsbit reported a major hack
invalid_events.description: Altsbit reported that a February 2020 hack caused significant cryptocurrency losses across multiple assets.
invalid_events.notes: Security incident that led to the final shutdown decision.

invalid_events.title: WEX stopped operating with customer funds unresolved
invalid_events.description: Public references describe WEX as ceasing work in late 2018 without returning customer assets, with later reporting and legal action referring to missing funds from WEX accounts.
invalid_events.notes: Approximate month-level terminal marker. Used because available public references point to late-2018 failure rather than a precise official shutdown notice.

invalid_events.title: Yapizon hack before rebrand
invalid_events.description: The exchange, then known as Yapizon, suffered a hack in 2017 before later rebranding to Youbit.
invalid_events.notes: Context event; not the terminal collapse.

invalid_events.title: Closed and filed for bankruptcy after second hack
invalid_events.description: Youbit announced it would close and file for bankruptcy after a second cyberattack caused another major asset loss.
invalid_events.notes: Used as death_date because the exchange announced closure and bankruptcy filing on this date.

invalid_events.title: Bter reported major BTC theft
invalid_events.description: Bter reported a major Bitcoin theft in February 2015, one of several incidents in the early exchange-security era.
invalid_events.notes: Security incident retained as context; not used as the terminal status reason.

invalid_events.title: Blockchain Global entered administration and liquidation process
invalid_events.description: Blockchain Global later entered voluntary administration, with liquidators and regulators examining debts, creditor claims, and conduct of former directors.
invalid_events.notes: Follow-up legal and administration event supporting insolvency classification.

invalid_events.title: LocalMonero final shutdown date reached
invalid_events.description: The official shutdown schedule set November 7, 2024 as the final website shutdown date after earlier trade and withdrawal deadlines.
invalid_events.notes: Used as death_date because the official schedule identified this as the final shutdown date.

invalid_source_type_evidence.notes: Official post stating that BTER users can move BTS funds to gate.io.

invalid_source_type_evidence.notes: Current exchange-status page marks Cryptonit as inactive.
